name: Scaffold a Go CLI
description: Greenfield Go task. Workspace starts empty. The agent must scaffold a minimal Go CLI that compiles cleanly with `go build ./...`.
prompt: You are starting a fresh Go module. From an empty workspace, scaffold a minimal Go CLI named `frameval-demo`. Create (at minimum): `go.mod` declaring module `frameval-demo` (Go 1.22+), and `main.go` implementing `package main` with a `main` function that parses a `-name` string flag using the standard `flag` package and prints `Hello, <name>!` to stdout. The project must build with `go build ./...` and no extra dependencies are allowed.
technical_details: Compile check: `go build ./...` must exit with code 0 in the generated workspace.

name: Scaffold an Express app
description: Greenfield Node.js task. Workspace starts empty. The agent must scaffold an Express server that starts without syntax errors.
prompt: From an empty workspace, scaffold a minimal Node.js Express application. Deliverables: (1) `package.json` declaring `express` as a dependency, (2) `server.js` that requires `express`, creates an app, defines `GET /api/v1/status` returning JSON `{ status: 'ok' }`, and listens on `process.env.PORT || 3000`. Do not start the server at module load time in a way that would block `node --check`. The file `server.js` must pass `node --check server.js` (syntax check).
technical_details: Deterministic check: `node --check server.js` must succeed (syntax only, no execution).

name: Scaffold a Python module
description: Greenfield Python task. Workspace starts empty. The agent must produce an importable Python package.
prompt: From an empty workspace, create a Python package named `frameval_demo` with the following layout: `frameval_demo/__init__.py` that defines `def greet(name: str) -> str: return f'Hello, {name}!'`. No other files are strictly required. The package must import cleanly with `python3 -c "import frameval_demo; print(frameval_demo.greet('frameval'))"` — the command must exit 0.
technical_details: Deterministic check: `python3 -c` import statement must succeed.

name: Extend octocat/Hello-World README
description: Brownfield task. The workspace is a shallow clone of `octocat/Hello-World`. The agent must add an `## About` section to README without breaking the file.
prompt: You are in a shallow clone of https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World. Add a new top-level section titled exactly `## About` to `README` (append it to the end of the file). The section body must contain at least one sentence describing what the project is. Do not delete or modify any other lines.
technical_details: Deterministic check verifies README still exists and now contains `## About`.

test_cases.name: README contains the new About section

name: Add route to expressjs/hello-world
description: Brownfield Node.js task. Clones the official Express hello-world example and asks the agent to add a `/health` route that still lets the file pass `node --check`.
prompt: This workspace is a shallow clone of the Express repository. Navigate to `examples/hello-world/index.js` and add a new route `app.get('/health', (req, res) => res.json({ status: 'ok' }))` BEFORE the existing `app.listen(...)` call. Do not remove any existing routes. The edited file must still pass `node --check examples/hello-world/index.js`.
technical_details: Deterministic check: the modified hello-world example must still be syntactically valid JavaScript.
